nodes.context: 19th-century Irish-American street gang active in Greenwich Village and New York waterfront during early to mid-1890s.
Much older gang that dominated Lower West Side Manhattan with Potashes until end of 1890s.
Emerging gang that displaced Potashes and Boodle Gang, controlling the area for the next decade.

edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s
edges.context: Potashes and Boodle Gang jointly dominated Lower West Side Manhattan.
Hudson Dusters are listed as rivals of Potashes in the infobox.

edges.evidence_quote: They and the much older Boodle Gang dominated Lower West Side Manhattan until the end of the 1890s when they were displaced by the then emerging Hudson Dusters
edges.context: Hudson Dusters displaced Boodle Gang from dominance in Lower West Side Manhattan.
